book_summary: Leviticus has chapters 1-25 complete in restored/source-audited draft and mystical companion layers. The book now covers offerings and reparation, priestly ordination and rupture, clean/unclean ritual states, body and illness safeguards, Day of Atonement, holiness and social justice, difficult sexual/penalty texts with anti-weaponization metadata, priestly holy-food and bodily-wholeness boundaries, the appointed-times calendar, continual lamp and bread of presence, proportional-justice material, and the Jubilee economy of land rest, return, redemption, debt release, and anti-exploitation.

chapters.notes: Land Sabbath, Jubilee release, property return, redemption, anti-interest support, debt-servitude release, anti-crushing rule, and foreign-slavery exception flagged as moral fracture.

translation_notes: Jubilee proclaims liberty/release to all inhabitants and periodically reverses land and kinship displacement.
The land belongs to YHWH; humans are sojourners and resident settlers with Divine, not absolute owners.
Sale price is tied to remaining harvest years, limiting speculative permanent ownership.
When a kinsperson becomes poor, strengthen them so they may live with you; interest and food-profit are prohibited.
Israelite debt-servitude must not become slave labor and ends by Jubilee.
The foreign-slavery exception in verses 44-46 is source-preserved as a serious moral limitation that must never be normalized or defended today.
Exodus liberation governs the chapter: those brought out from Egypt belong to YHWH, not to human masters.
Mystical companion layer remains interpretive and does not replace source-audited restored text.
source_witness_audit_flags: Leviticus 25 land-Sabbath and Jubilee require comparison with Exodus and Deuteronomy debt-release traditions.
Leviticus 25 deror/liberty terminology requires lexical and ancient Near Eastern release-proclamation audit.
Leviticus 25 land ownership and redemption require agrarian, kinship, and anti-colonial audit.
Leviticus 25 interest/increase prohibitions require economic-history and predatory-lending audit.
Leviticus 25 Israelite debt-servitude requires anti-slavery and labor-history audit.
Leviticus 25:44-46 foreign slavery requires explicit anti-slavery reception-history metadata and categorical rejection as modern ethics.
Leviticus 25 Jubilee practice/history requires Second Temple and later Jewish reception audit.
next_actions: Continue Leviticus with chapter 26: covenant blessings and curses, land Sabbath consequences, exile, confession, remembrance, and strong anti-trauma/anti-abuse safeguards.
